StudentProfile.title: StudentProfile
StudentProfile.description: Represents a student user's profile information, excluding authentication details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student, used for identification.

languagePreference.description: The preferred language code of the student (e.g., 'en', 'es').

assignedTeacherId.description: Reference to the TeacherProfile entity if this student is assigned to a specific teacher. (Relationship: TeacherProfile 1:N StudentProfile)

TeacherProfile.title: TeacherProfile
TeacherProfile.description: Represents a teacher user's profile information, excluding authentication details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TeacherProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the teacher.

email.description: The email address of the teacher, used for identification.

LearningModule.title: LearningModule
LearningModule.description: Represents a structured learning module or course, available in a specific language.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningModule entity.

title.description: The title of the learning module.

description.description: A brief description of the learning module's content and objectives.

subject.description: The subject area this module belongs to (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'Science').

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this module within a curriculum or subject category.

languageCode.description: The language code for this version of the learning module (e.g., 'en', 'es').

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents an individual lesson within a learning module, available in a specific language.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

learningModuleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N Lesson)

content.description: The main educational content or text of the lesson.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this lesson within its learning module.

languageCode.description: The language code for this version of the lesson (e.g., 'en', 'es').

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents an interactive question presented to students, available in a specific language.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this question is associated with. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Question)

questionText.description: The text of the question posed to the student.

options.description: An array of possible answer options for 'quiz' type questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer to the question, or the ID of the correct option for quiz questions.

difficultyLevel.description: An integer representing the difficulty level of the question (e.g., 1-5).

languageCode.description: The language code for this version of the question (e.g., 'en', 'es').

StudentResponse.title: StudentResponse
StudentResponse.description: Records a student's answer or attempt for a specific question.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentResponse entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile who submitted this response. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N StudentResponse)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question to which this response applies. (Relationship: Question 1:N StudentResponse)

responseContent.description: The actual content of the student's response (e.g., text input, selected option ID, voice transcription).

submittedAt.description: The timestamp when the student submitted the response.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the student's response was objectively correct.

score.description: A numeric score assigned to the response, typically between 0 and 1.

aiEvaluationStatus.description: The AI's classification of the student's understanding (e.g., 'correct', 'incorrect', 'partial_knowledge', 'confused', 'guessing').

aiConfidenceScore.description: A numeric value (0-1) indicating the AI's confidence in its evaluation of the response.

Explanation.title: Explanation
Explanation.description: Represents an AI-generated explanation provided to a student based on their response, available in a specific language.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Explanation entity.

studentResponseId.description: Reference to the StudentResponse this explanation is for. (Relationship: StudentResponse 1:1 Explanation)

generatedText.description: The text content of the AI-generated explanation.

imageUrl.description: An optional URL to an image or visual aid accompanying the explanation.

languageCode.description: The language code of the generated explanation (e.g., 'en', 'es').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the explanation was generated.

LearningProgress.title: LearningProgress
LearningProgress.description: Tracks a student's progress within a specific learning module.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningProgress entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the StudentProfile whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: StudentProfile 1:N LearningProgress)

learningModuleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule this progress record is for. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N LearningProgress)

currentLessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson the student is currently working on or last accessed within this module. (Relationship: Lesson 1:1 LearningProgress)

completedLessonIds.description: An array of IDs of lessons that the student has completed within this module. (References to Lesson)

lastActivityAt.description: The timestamp of the student's last activity within this module.

completionPercentage.description: The percentage of the module completed by the student (0-100).

overallScore.description: The student's aggregated score for all completed questions within this module.

Misconception.title: Misconception
Misconception.description: Aggregated data representing common misconceptions identified among students, relevant for teacher insights.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Misconception entity.

learningModuleId.description: Reference to the LearningModule where this misconception is observed. (Relationship: LearningModule 1:N Misconception)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson where this misconception is most frequently encountered. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Misconception)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question that often triggers this misconception. (Relationship: Question 1:N Misconception)

misconceptionDescription.description: A description of the common misunderstanding or error.

commonIncorrectResponses.description: An array of common incorrect answers or response patterns observed for this misconception.

studentCountAffected.description: The number of students who have demonstrated this specific misconception.

severityLevel.description: The perceived severity or impact of this misconception (e.g., 'low', 'medium', 'high').

detectedAt.description: The timestamp when this misconception was first detected or last updated.

languageCode.description: The language code relevant to this misconception data (e.g., 'en', 'es').

definition.description: Stores individual student profiles, accessible only by the owning student (`studentId` matches `request.auth.uid`). Includes `assignedTeacherId` for referencing the student's assigned teacher.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual teacher profiles, accessible only by the owning teacher (`teacherId` matches `request.auth.uid`). The existence of a document here identifies a user as a teacher.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier of the teacher, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection for all learning modules. Read access is global. Write access is restricted to authorized teachers/admins.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a learning module.

definition.description: Subcollection of learning modules, storing individual lessons. Inherits public read access from its parent module.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent learning module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for a lesson within the module.

definition.description: Subcollection of lessons, storing questions. Inherits public read access from its parent lesson and module.

params.name: learningModuleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent learning module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent lesson.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a question within the lesson.

definition.description: Stores student responses to questions. Includes denormalized `studentId` and `assignedTeacherId` for authorization independence. Accessible by the student owner and their assigned teacher.

params.name: studentResponseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a student's response.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated explanations for student responses. Includes denormalized `studentId` and `assignedTeacherId` for authorization independence. Accessible by the student owner and their assigned teacher.

params.name: explanationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an AI-generated explanation.

definition.description: Tracks student progress within learning modules. Includes denormalized `studentId` and `assignedTeacherId` for authorization independence. Accessible by the student owner and their assigned teacher.

params.name: learningProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for a student's learning progress record.

definition.description: Aggregated data on common student misconceptions, primarily for teacher insights. Readable by authenticated teachers. Write access is restricted to Cloud Functions.

params.name: misconceptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for an identified misconception.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for ShikshaSetu prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and complicate debugging, authorization-relevant fields are denormalized. Specifically:

1.  **Student-specific Data (`StudentResponse`, `Explanation`, `LearningProgress`):** These entities are primarily owned by a student but may also need to be accessible by an assigned teacher. Therefore, the `studentId` (corresponding to `request.auth.uid`) and the `assignedTeacherId` (denormalized from the `StudentProfile` of the `studentId`) are explicitly included in each document. This allows for direct authorization checks within rules, like `request.auth.uid == resource.data.studentId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.assignedTeacherId`, without needing to fetch parent documents.
2.  **User Profiles (`StudentProfile`, `TeacherProfile`):** These are top-level collections, directly identified by the user's UID (`studentId` or `teacherId`), making access control straightforward (`request.auth.uid == studentId`). The `assignedTeacherId` in `StudentProfile` is a reference, not for direct rule authorization on the StudentProfile itself, but for denormalization into student-generated data.

**Structural Segregation & Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs):**

1.  **Public Content (`LearningModule`, `Lesson`, `Question`):** These collections represent static, curriculum content that is globally readable by all users (students, teachers, even unauthenticated users if desired, though the app implies logged-in users). Their hierarchical structure (`/learningModules/{learningModuleId}/lessons/{lessonId}/questions/{questionId}`) is efficient for organizing content, and since all documents within these collections share a 'public read' security posture, security rules are simple and broad (`allow read: true`). This inherently supports QAPs as any list query on these collections is secure.
2.  **Private User Data (`StudentProfile`, `TeacherProfile`):** These are top-level collections. `StudentProfile` documents are owned by the respective `studentId`, and `TeacherProfile` documents by `teacherId`. QAPs for these are trivial: a student queries their own profile, a teacher queries their own profile. For teachers querying students, a teacher's `teacherProfile` can list `assignedStudentIds`, and then the teacher can query `studentProfiles` where `id` is in `assignedStudentIds` (requiring `get()` in the rule or denormalization if individual student profiles need to be read without a `get()`). The primary goal is teacher *insights*.
3.  **Collaborative/Private-but-shared Data (`StudentResponse`, `Explanation`, `LearningProgress`):** These are top-level collections to allow for flexible querying by both `studentId` and `assignedTeacherId`. For students, listing their own responses or progress uses `where('studentId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. For teachers, listing responses/progress for their students uses `where('assignedTeacherId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. This design ensures that all `list` operations are implicitly secured by the `where` clause matching the `request.auth.uid` against a denormalized field, satisfying QAPs.
4.  **Teacher-Specific Insights (`Misconception`):** This collection is designed to aggregate data for teachers. It's readable by any authenticated user who is identified as a teacher (i.e., `exists(/teacherProfiles/request.auth.uid)`). This segregation ensures students cannot access this aggregate data, while teachers can perform list queries without needing complex filtering, supporting QAPs.

This structure ensures a clear separation of concerns, simplifies security rules dramatically, and enables efficient, secure querying for all defined user roles and data types, aligning with the core principles for a robust Firestore design.
